SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.service.timeline

Class GameTimelineManager

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.service.timeline.GameTimelineManager
public class GameTimelineManager extends Object
Centralized manager for all game-related timelines (game loop, elapsed time, level countdown). Provides unified pause/resume/stop operations.
Author:
Dong, Jia

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameTimelineManager()
    Constructs a manager without callbacks.
    GameTimelineManager(Runnable moveDownCallback, Runnable timeUpdateCallback, IntConsumer levelTickCallback)
    Constructs a GameTimelineManager with custom callbacks.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    isGameLoopRunning()
    Checks if the game loop timeline is currently running.
    void
    pauseAll()
    Pauses all running timelines (game loop, elapsed time, level countdown).
    void
    reinitializeTimelines()
    Reinitializes all timelines.
    void
    resumeAll()
    Resumes all paused timelines (game loop, elapsed time, level countdown).
    void
    setCallbacks(Runnable moveDownCallback, Runnable timeUpdateCallback, IntConsumer levelTickCallback)
    Sets or updates all callbacks.
    void
    startElapsedTimer(long startTime)
    Starts elapsed time timer.
    void
    startGameLoop(int speed)
    Starts the game loop with specified speed.
    void
    startLevelCountdown(int remainingSeconds)
    Starts level countdown timer.
    void
    stopAll()
    Stops all timelines completely.
    void
    stopElapsedTimer()
    Stops the elapsed time timer.
    void
    stopGameLoop()
    Stops the game loop timeline.
    void
    stopLevelTimer()
    Stops the level countdown timer.
    void
    updateGameLoopSpeed(int newSpeed)
    Updates game loop speed without stopping if running.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • GameTimelineManager

      public GameTimelineManager()
      Constructs a manager without callbacks. Use setCallbacks() before starting timelines.

    • GameTimelineManager

      public GameTimelineManager(Runnable moveDownCallback, Runnable timeUpdateCallback, IntConsumer levelTickCallback)
      Constructs a GameTimelineManager with custom callbacks.
      Parameters:
      moveDownCallback - game loop callback
      timeUpdateCallback - elapsed time callback
      levelTickCallback - level countdown callback

  • Method Details

    • setCallbacks

      public void setCallbacks(Runnable moveDownCallback, Runnable timeUpdateCallback, IntConsumer levelTickCallback)
      Sets or updates all callbacks.
      Parameters:
      moveDownCallback - game loop callback (can be null)
      timeUpdateCallback - elapsed time callback (can be null)
      levelTickCallback - level countdown callback (can be null)

    • startGameLoop

      public void startGameLoop(int speed)
      Starts the game loop with specified speed.
      Parameters:
      speed - drop speed in milliseconds (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if speed <= 0
      IllegalStateException - if gameTimeline or moveDownCallback is null

    • stopGameLoop

      public void stopGameLoop()
      Stops the game loop timeline. Prevents automatic brick falling until restarted.

    • updateGameLoopSpeed

      public void updateGameLoopSpeed(int newSpeed)
      Updates game loop speed without stopping if running.
      Parameters:
      newSpeed - new drop speed in milliseconds (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if newSpeed <= 0
      IllegalStateException - if gameTimeline or moveDownCallback is null

    • startElapsedTimer

      public void startElapsedTimer(long startTime)
      Starts elapsed time timer.
      Parameters:
      startTime - start time in milliseconds (reserved for future use)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if elapsedTimeTimer or timeUpdateCallback is null

    • stopElapsedTimer

      public void stopElapsedTimer()
      Stops the elapsed time timer. Prevents further time updates until restarted.

    • startLevelCountdown

      public void startLevelCountdown(int remainingSeconds)
      Starts level countdown timer. Auto-stops at 0.
      Parameters:
      remainingSeconds - initial remaining seconds (must be non-negative)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if remainingSeconds < 0
      IllegalStateException - if levelTimer or levelTickCallback is null

    • stopLevelTimer

      public void stopLevelTimer()
      Stops the level countdown timer. Prevents further countdown updates until restarted.

    • pauseAll

      public void pauseAll()
      Pauses all running timelines (game loop, elapsed time, level countdown). Game state is preserved and can be resumed.

    • resumeAll

      public void resumeAll()
      Resumes all paused timelines (game loop, elapsed time, level countdown). Restores gameplay from paused state.

    • stopAll

      public void stopAll()
      Stops all timelines completely. Must be called on JavaFX Application Thread.

    • reinitializeTimelines

      public void reinitializeTimelines()
      Reinitializes all timelines. Call this after stopAll() to create fresh Timeline instances. Must be called on JavaFX Application Thread.

    • isGameLoopRunning

      public boolean isGameLoopRunning()
      Checks if the game loop timeline is currently running.
      Returns:
      true if the game loop is running, false otherwise
